coredata.dc:title: Advances in ultrasonic production units for enhanced oil recovery in China
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Zhenjun
Fang, Ri
Guo, Hangyuan
coredata.dc:description: With the development of oil recovery technology, ultrasonic technology has been involved in oil production and oilfield development. The mechanism of ultrasonic wave plugging in near well is different from the conventional oil recovery technology. Ultrasonic oil production technique is an effective method to enhance oil production with low cost, good applicability, and no environmental pollution. The core part of ultrasonic oil production equipment for Enhanced Oil Recovery is a high-power ultrasonic transducer. The continuous high-power ultrasound is used to treat the reservoir, which changes the pore structure, the physical property and the state of the fluid, thus improving the permeability and flows conditions of the reservoir, and increasing the oil yield and oil recovery. Ultrasonic oil recovery equipment includes the generation of high-power ultrasonic signals, long-distance transmission and the conversion of electrical energy to acoustic energy. In this paper, state-of-the-art on the development of ultrasonic oil production devices for Enhanced Oil Recovery in China is introduced. The purpose of this paper is to provide a reference for the development of high-power ultrasonic oil extraction equipment and its promotion in tertiary oil recovery technology.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ultrasonic
Oilfield
Enhance oil recovery

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sectiontitle sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure table e-component body acknowledge affil appendices articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast footnotes grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst primabst ref

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: The influence of different factors on oil recovery efficiency of ultrasonic treatment.
The recent development of ultrasonic oil production equipment
Development trend of ultrasonic oil recovery technology

xocs:cp-license-lines.xocs:cp-license-line: This is an open access article under the CC BY-NC-ND license.

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This research was supported by the Shanghai Natural Science Foundation under the grant number of 18ZR1426700 . Appendix A

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The schematic diagram of ultrasonic oil recovery [6]. 1) oil reservoir; 2) ultrasonic downhole equipment; 3) packer; 4) tubing; 5) casing valve; 6) lubricator; 7) flowout line; 8) cable feed; 9) cable; 10)wire line truckПКC-5; 11) pump unitCИH-32, 12-house.

The structure of a kind of environmentally-friendly, anti-wax and anti-scaling viscosity reduction device 1-cyclone, 2-jet ultrasonic transponder, 3-jet ultrasonic oscillator, 4-emo cavitation chamber, 5-tube.
The structure of electromagnetic ultrasonic anti-scaling device 1-inner tube, 2-flange, 3-front ring, 4-amplifier, 5-amplification rod, 6-transducer, 7-rear coil.

The structure of double sonic eddy current anti-wax viscosity reduction device 1-jet head, 2 -vibrating shrapnel, 3- bracket, 4-empty chamber, 5-spiral groove, 6-seat, 7-ball, 8- spiral groove, 9-jet section, 10-flow section, 11-shell, 12-wedge-shaped through-hole.
Three kind of special cables for improving transmission efficiency of the whole oil production system.

The structure of a ultrasonic radiation device for recycle OilRecoveryWastewater 1-inner tube, 2-filter, 3-stainless steel round tube body, 4-connecting flange, 5-accommodating cavity, 6-fixing bolt, 7-piezoelectric ceramic plate, 8-electrode piece, 9-ultrasonic reflection block, 10-cable, 11-outlet, 12-silicone tapered seal, 13-fastening nut, 14-high temperature insulation film.
The structure diagram of lithium niobate piezoelectric vibrator.
The latest typical ultrasonic oil recovery equipment developed in China.

row.entry: A kind of environmentally-friendly, anti-wax and anti-scaling viscosity reduction device

This invention overcomes the problems that the existing similar products have a poor effect on wax prevention and viscosity reduction. It truly has a strong anti-wax viscosity and anti-scaling function. Field test results indicate that it can increase the flow of crude oil greatly, improve pump efficiency and increase production of oil wells. It has apparent advantages, such as safety, non-pollution and without additional power. In a word, this device is not only suitable for different types of oil wells, but for oil wells with different wax content and water content.
Test results show that the advantages of the device are good scale removal effect, good antifouling effect, long life length, no pollution, convenient maintenance, and non-environmental pollution.
a kind of downhole ultrasonic oil production transducer with matching device

Field test results indicate that it cannot only improve the transmission efficiency of the transmission system greatly (the transmission efficiency of the existing transmission system can be improved up to 70%) but can release most of the trapped oil, dredge clogged oil passages and increase the amount of mining. The device has the advantages of high transmission efficiency and energy saving.
Apparatus and method for measuring downhole rock mechanical parameters under ultrasonic and chemical action

Test results show that the device has the advantages of reasonable design, convenient installation, perfect function, and convenient operation, in addition, the equipment can quickly complete the measurement of rock mechanics parameters under the combined action of ultrasonic and chemical reagents.
A High-power ultrasonic oil recovery device

The equipment cannot generate pollution and damage to the oil layer, meets the requirements of environmental protection and the sustainable development of oil fields, and has the advantages of low cost, quick effect, and high benefit.
Double sonic eddy current anti-wax viscosity reduction device

The device can reduce the viscosity of crude oil, prevent wax deposition, increase its flow capacity and increase the production of oil wells. It is suitable for production wells of various vertical, inclined and horizontal wells.

The invention can realize the efficient coupling of the ultrasonic cavitation and the hydrodynamic cavitation processing technology and has much potential application values in the fields of energy, environmental protection, chemical industry, and medicine.
A device for the extraction of heavy oil based on the combination of sound, light and electricity

Research indicates that the recovery rate can be improved by about 1–5%, using this invention, in addition, the invention has the following advantages: small footprint, suitable for all types of the reservoir exploitation in various types and stages, and simple process, low energy consumption, no pollution, having the ability to clear the plug, good orientation, convenient operation, and easy management.

head.ce:title: Advances in ultrasonic production units for enhanced oil recovery in China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Business Administration, Wonkwang University, 460 Iksandae-ro, Iksan, Jeonbuk 54538, South Korea

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: With the development of oil recovery technology, ultrasonic technology has been involved in oil production and oilfield development. The mechanism of ultrasonic wave plugging in near well is different from the conventional oil recovery technology. Ultrasonic oil production technique is an effective method to enhance oil production with low cost, good applicability, and no environmental pollution. The core part of ultrasonic oil production equipment for Enhanced Oil Recovery is a high-power ultrasonic transducer. The continuous high-power ultrasound is used to treat the reservoir, which changes the pore structure, the physical property and the state of the fluid, thus improving the permeability and flows conditions of the reservoir, and increasing the oil yield and oil recovery. Ultrasonic oil recovery equipment includes the generation of high-power ultrasonic signals, long-distance transmission and the conversion of electrical energy to acoustic energy. In this paper, state-of-the-art on the development of ultrasonic oil production devices for Enhanced Oil Recovery in China is introduced. The purpose of this paper is to provide a reference for the development of high-power ultrasonic oil extraction equipment and its promotion in tertiary oil recovery technology.

ce:section.ce:para: Energy is the basic condition for the development of human society. In the last two hundred years, coal, oil, and natural gas are the basic system of energy, which promotes the development of human society and civilization [1].

Today, the oil and gas industry is facing severe challenges: the total demand for oil and gas energy is accelerating, the oil production capacity cannot keep up with the consumption rhythm, and the contradiction between supply and demand is becoming increasingly obvious; under the trend of oil production by science and technology, the cost of oil production by various oil companies has increased. In the face of high oil prices, the pressure on the oil market is great, and the international political structure is complicated, which affects the normal progress of oil cooperation [1–4].

In order to get rid of these difficulties, ultrasonic oil recovery technology has been studied in recent years. This technique was developed in the Soviet Union and the United States in the 1950s [5]. Ultrasonic wave has shown the characteristics of low pollution, low cost and high efficiency for compact reservoirs.

In oil exploitation, impurities such as sediment existing in the formation will cause blockage of pores in the near well, which will greatly reduce the flow capacity of oil and lead to a decrease in production [6]. When high-power ultrasonic waves act on the oil layer, the sound wave propagation process will generate a large acceleration, which can destroy the particle structure of the oil layer and achieve the purpose of increasing oil permeability [7].

Ultrasonic oil recovery technology [3–5] refers to the use of ultrasonic waves to treat near-well oil layers in water injection wells and production oil wells, by changing the physical property and state of the fluid in the reservoir, improving the circulation condition and permeability of the near well reservoir at the bottom of the well, and solving the plugging of the injection wells and oil wells, the purpose of increasing the crude oil production, water injection rate and oil recovery is achieved [6].

Ultrasonic oil production equipment is mostly vehicle-mounted, mainly composed of ultrasonic detector, an ultrasonic generator, high-power armored coaxial cable, and piezoelectric ceramic transducer. The schematic diagram of ultrasonic oil recovery is shown in Fig. 1
[6].

The advantages of ultrasonic oil recovery are [6,7]: (1) no environmental pollution. When ultrasonic waves are applied to an oil well layer, they do not damage the formation or pollute the environment. For residual oil that cannot be produced, it can wait for later mining with more advanced technology, without wasting resources, and the technique meets the requirements of sustainable development; (2) ultrasonic treatment process is simple and easy to operate. At present, the most commonly used ultrasonic oil recovery equipment is car-mounted type. In actual use, only one cable car and four or five workers are needed, which can save costs; (3) combining ultrasonic oil recovery technology with other development methods (such as sonic water injection and sonic acidification or stimulation measures) can further increase crude oil production and recovery; (4) ultrasonic oil recovery equipment also has the advantages of wide application, fastereconomiceffect, and high efficiency.

The theoretical research and technical development of acoustic oil production in the United States began around 1950, and the research in acoustic oil recovery field has not been interrupted until now [8]. In the 1960s, Professor Robert Verbeck of the University of West Virginia in the United States made a good start for the development of ultrasonic oil recovery technology by measuring the permeability of acoustic waves through rock oil. Duhon studied the effect of ultrasonic vibration energy on fluid flow in porous media for the first time in 1964 [9–11]. It was found that the fluid flow characteristics were different under the action of the ultrasonic wave at different frequencies [12,13]. The conclusion is that ultrasonic wave can improve water drive recovery. He proposed for the first time that ultrasonic propagation would change the structure of crude oil and reduce its viscosity [14,15]. Venkitaraman [16] uses ultrasonic waves with different frequency energies to act on the core contaminated by the drilling fluid. The results show that the permeability of core is significantly increased by ultrasonic wave. Gollapudi et al. [17] have carried out an experimental study on asphalt sediments by ultrasonic wave. It is considered that ultrasonic cavitation and thermal action are the fundamental reasons for reducing the viscosity of crude oil and enhancing the flow capacity of crude oil, and this mechanism is used to explain the excellent effect of ultrasonic plugging. The permeability of the reservoir increases after the plugging is removed, thus increasing the flow rate of the oil [17]. According to the theory put forward by previous scientists, Champion compares the degree of near well clogging before and after ultrasonic action with the results of field tests, and verifies its effectiveness, and tests the effect of fluid seepage [18,19]. The results show that ultrasonic radiation can improve fluid flow velocity. The cavitation effect generated by ultrasonic wave in the original, as well as the absorption inside the medium, the friction action at the interface and boundary of different media will release a lot of heat. If the intensity of the sound field is high, a lot of energy in the wellbore is converted into heat. Thermal action can increase the temperature of oil and reduce the viscosity of crude oil. As the viscosity decreases, the interaction force between molecules in the fluid and the resistance to fluid flow become smaller. This is beneficial to increase the flow rate of crude oil in the formation and is beneficial to improve water flooding efficiency and oil recovery. Tom W. Bakker et al. [20] studied the ultrasonic cavitation alone and theoretically derived the environmental conditions for cavitation in the well. Poesio et al. [23] has mainly studied the change of fluid pressure in porous media after the ultrasonic wave. The results show that ultrasonic wave can reduce the pressure gradient of porous media, and then the acoustic flow phenomenon of a porous medium under ultrasonic irradiation is experimentally studied. Sau Wai Wong puts forward several key problems of ultrasonic high-efficiency oil recovery according to the effect of the ultrasonic wave on near well zone [24]. Jose Gil Cidoncha enumerates some field application examples, describes the development course of ultrasonic oil recovery technology, develops the ultrasonic oil recovery mechanism according to the existing conclusions, and forecasts the future trend of this technology [25,26]. Roberts [25,27] has shown that ultrasonic energy can effectively remove sediments and restore the effective permeability of rocks.

With the development of ultrasonic technology, there are more and more kinds of ultrasonic transducers, and the power is increasing. Especially for the ultrasonic transducer with longitudinal vibration mode, the design theory is basically perfect. In terms of transducer theory, W.P. Mason put forward an equivalent network analysis method in the 1940s [28] and developed this theory into a transmission matrix method for the analysis of one-dimensional longitudinal vibration of complex structures for a long time. In 1970, Sen Rongsi first proposed the apparent elastic method in the analysis of two-dimensional vibration problems [29], which became the commonly used finite element and boundary element method for analyzing three-dimensional vibration problems.

It was only in the 1950s that China began to study high-power ultrasonic transducers and their theories, beginning with ultrasonic processing, cleaning, dropping, crushing and emulsifying, and further studying magnetostrictive transducers [30]. After the 1960s, the main attention will be devoted to the study of the sandwich piezoelectric transducer and the establishment of one-dimensional theory using the equivalent network. In the development and optimization of the transducer, the resonance frequency and efficiency of the transducer with stress, electric load and loss are first given, the parameters determining the maximum efficiency of the transducer are proposed, and the optimum design direction is given. Two-dimensional analysis of piezoelectric transducers for large-sized piezoelectric transducers has been carried out in China for the last two decades, and the design method of piezoelectric transducers for composite vibration is described [31].

Ultrasonic oil recovery technology has been widely used in the United States and Russia, but has been technically blocked. In recent years, China's ultrasonic oil recovery technology has been greatly developed. Many new equipments have been carried out on-site operations in some oil fields in China, such as Daqing and Shengli Oilfields, and achieved good results. The purpose of this paper is to promote the further development of this technology by reviewing China's oil recovery technology.

Ultrasound treatment for oil reservoir is to put high-power ultrasonic transducer down to the position of oil reservoir, Under the functions of mechanical, cavitation, heat, sound flow, etc., the plug is loosened and shed, and discharged from the well with liquid. Ultrasound has the following effects on reservoir and stratum:

Ultrasonic wave is the transmitter of mechanical vibration in elastic medium. In the process of propagation, the amplitude and velocity of elastic particles change significantly, resulting in stirring, loosening, boundary friction, dispersion, fatigue damage, micro-cracks, degassing, acoustic fogging, disaggregation, impact fragmentation and thermal action. (a) Mechanical vibration can destroy the cohesion between the plugged particles and the reservoir rock; (b) Mechanical vibration changes the capillary pore size, reducing the surface tension of the pores, resulting in expansion and contraction of the capillary; (c) The impact pressure produced by mechanical vibration causes micro-cracks in formation rocks. When the vibration reaches a certain intensity, the affinity between crude oil and rock is weakened, and the crude oil is separated from rock mass; (d) The boundary friction exerts a local heating effect on the fluid. The local heating effect of boundary friction is one of the sources of ultrasonic thermal effect. Boundary friction occurs locally and is intense, often producing local high temperature. For crude oils with higher viscosity, the absorption coefficient is large, and the local temperature rise caused by it is very considerable; (e) Reduce the viscosity of crude oil and increase the seepage velocity. Crude oil is a fluid containing waxy, colloid, asphaltene and other macromolecule compounds. Under the action of ultrasonic waves with high frequency and high intensity, mechanical vibration causes large molecules to have large acceleration, forming relative motion between molecules. Due to the inertia of the molecule, the molecular chain is broken and the macromolecule is pulverized, especially in the cavitation state, and this depolymerization is more obvious. For the polymer compound in the crude oil, the depolymerization is sufficiently pulverized to reduce the viscosity of the crude oil. Of course, this can only happen at high sound intensity and over long periods of time. The high temperature produced by vibration can also reduce viscosity.

Ultrasonic cavitation refers to the process of the growth and collapse of liquid hollow bubbles under the action of ultrasound, as well as a series of sound pressure changes accompanying them. Theoretical studies show that high pressure over 105 MPa can be generated in the local space of cavity collapse [39]. It can also cause a series of secondary effects, such as luminescence, phonation, ionization, and chemical reactions. Cavitation is inhibited at high pressure, but it can be promoted by the presence of dissolved gas and particle contamination in the fluid. (a) Cavitation causes changes in sound pressure and eliminates air resistance. Almost all liquids contain dissolved or bubble-formed gases at room temperature and pressure. Crude oil dissolves more hydrocarbons under formation pressure. The existence of a large number of gas nuclei has a great impediment to the flow of oil, which is called gas resistance. When the eigenfrequency of the gas core resonates with the sound frequency, the gas core undergoes strong vibration. A large number of gas cores are rapidly combined in the sound field to form large bubbles, and the continuously expanding bubbles quickly break away from the capillary channels to achieve the purpose of dredging [40]; (b) Cavitation produces shock waves when the gas core collapses, and the sawtooth wave formed when the large amplitude vibration propagates in the medium is a periodic shock wave. The gas inside the bubble is hot-compressed, and the temperature can reach several thousand degrees. The shock generated during the collapse has a strong impact on the surroundings, which greatly enhances the mechanical action of the ultrasonic wave. Repeated cavitation bursts occur in formation fractures or on solid surfaces, and the instantaneous high pressures caused by these bursts blast away particles that adhere to the surface. Because of the alternation and transverse flow of liquids, particles are quickly removed from the surface [32]; (c) Cavitation can reduce the relative molecular mass of crude oil. Cavitation can break the molecular bonds of crude oil and reduce its relative molecular mass, thus reducing its viscosity and improving its fluidity. When the acoustic wave acts on the reservoir, the strong cavitation effect of crude oil will also cause local high temperature and high pressure instantaneously. This promotes chemical effects such as oxidation-reduction reaction, polymerization or depolymerization of macromolecule substances, and fracture of bonded asphaltene molecular bonds, so as to achieve the goal of oil layer plugging removal [42]; (d) Cavitation can produce a series of secondary effects. In the sound field, the bubbles in the liquid gradually form, expand, and then suddenly burst. During the rapid collapse of the bubble, high temperature and high pressure appear in the bubble, and a local strong shock wave is also formed in the liquid near the bubble. This also produces a range of secondary effects such as chemical effects, sonoluminescence, dispersion and emulsification. They all have a corresponding effect on the formation.

The resistance of the oil body flowing is mainly caused by the internal friction in the crude oil. The concentration of crude oil decreases with the decrease of viscosity. Low concentration results in lower flow resistance of crude oil. Therefore, one of the main measures to improve the velocity of oil/gas seepage is to increase oil temperature and reduce viscosity. The thermal effect of ultrasound is a comprehensive effect. It includes the whole heating caused by absorption, the local heating at the boundary surface and the local heating at the wavefront when the shock wave is formed. It is mainly manifested in the following three aspects: (a) The absorption of ultrasonic waves inside the propagation medium causes the acoustic energy to be converted into thermal energy; (b) at the interface of different media, boundary friction can increase the temperature of crude oil; (c) cavitation releases a large amount of heat energy at the moment of bubble collapse, and the local temperature can reach thousands of degrees Celsius. With the increase of ultrasonic frequency, the absorption effect and boundary friction become better and more intense. As the intensity of the ultrasound increases, cavitation and thermal effect become stronger and more significant.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The influence of different factors on oil recovery efficiency of ultrasonic treatment.
ce:section.ce:para: Recently, many studies have been carried out to explore the effects of different factors on the efficiency of ultrasonic oil recovery technology. It has been found that the oil separation rate after ultrasonic treatment is 55.6%; the optimum temperature, power, and frequency for oil recovery from sludge is 40 °C, 0.1 MPa and 28 kHz respectively [67]. In addition, the optimum condition for treating oily sludge by ultrasonic wave is that ultrasonic frequency, intensity, power, and the soil/water ratio are 25 kHz, 0.33 W/cm2, 300 W, and 1/2 respectively [68]. It is reported that the oil recovery rate can reach 95% when ultrasonic frequency, processing time, power, and temperature are 28 kHz, 15 min, 400 W and 60 °C respectively, when the ultrasonic power increases to more than 400 W, the oil recovery rate will not increase [69].

Fabiane G. Antes et al. [21] carried out a comprehensive study for the evaluation of ultrasound (US) frequency for demulsification of crude oil emulsions. Results show that crude oil demulsification is achieved at frequencies in the range of 25–45 kHz for all tested emulsions; no changes in the characteristics of the crude oil emulsions are observed when the applied frequencies are higher than 45 kHz. From the perspective of sustainable development, it is still a research hotspot to recover crude oil from oily sludge. Ultrasonic technology has been proved to be an efficient and environmentally friendly technology. To explore the influence of sludge characteristics on the efficiency of ultrasound-assisted oil recovery, He, Shilong et al. [22] conducted the analysis of variance (ANOVA) on six types of crude oily sludge with hydrophilicity and lipophilicity separately based on five different ultrasonic operation factors (ultrasonic power, frequency, ultrasonic treatment time, initial temperature and pH). Results shows that the highest 92% of oil recovery rate is achieved when the applied ultrasonic power is 240 W and the contained sludge is hydrophilic. The result proves that oil recovery efficiency is mainly affected by the ultrasonic power and hydrophilicity of sludge. Xin Zhang et al. [70] studied removing calcium carbonate plug from near wellbore by high-power ultrasonic treatment. Results show that the transducer frequency, transducer power, ultrasonic treatment time and initial permeability of core have great influence on the effect of ultrasonic scale removal; when the cumulative ultrasonic treatment time exceeds 60 min, the core permeability recovery rate tends to be stable; the effect is best when cumulative ultrasonic treatment time is 80–120 min. In addition, results indicate that the optimum ultrasonic frequency range for removing near wellbore plugging caused by the chemical precipitation of inorganic salts is within 20–25 kHz; with the increase of ultrasonic transducer power, the plugging removal effect is improved; with the increase of ultrasonic transducer frequency, the plugging removal effect is improved, but with the continuous increase of frequency and power, the plugging removal effect cannot be further improved. In a word, ultrasonic frequency and power should be controlled within a reasonable range.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The recent development of ultrasonic oil production equipment
ce:section.ce:para: Currently, in fact, the most commonly used transducers in the field of acoustic oil recovery are the transducers made of PZT. However, such piezoelectric ceramic transducers are prone to a large capacitive resistance in a transmission system consisting of ultrasonic power sources, transmission cables, and piezoelectric ceramic transducers, so that the entire transmission system can produce a larger reactive current, greatly reducing the transmission system transmission efficiency and resulting in a great waste of energy. According to the survey, the transmission efficiency of the existing transmission system is only 10% to 20%. In order to overcome these problems, Dekun Luo et al. [54] invented a kind of downhole ultrasonic oil production transducer with a matching device. Field test results indicate that it cannot only improve the transmission efficiency of the transmission system greatly (the transmission efficiency of the existing transmission system can be improved up to 70%) but can release most of the trapped oil, dredge clogged oil passages and increase the amount of mining. The device has the advantages of high transmission efficiency and energy saving [54].

Biqi Wang et al. [55] invented a kind of environmentally-friendly, anti-wax and anti-scaling viscosity reduction device used for underground oil pumping, its structure is shown in Fig. 2
[55]. This invention overcomes the problems that the existing similar products have a poor effect on wax prevention and viscosity reduction. It truly has a strong anti-wax viscosity and anti-scaling function. Field test results indicate that it can increase the flow of crude oil greatly, improve pump efficiency and increase production of oil wells. It has apparent advantages, such as safety, non-pollution and without additional power. In a word, this device is not only suitable for different types of oil wells, but for oil wells with different wax content and water content.

The scale removal of oil pipelines has always been a difficult problem in the petroleum industry. In order to solve this problem, Zebing Zhang et al. [56] invented an electromagnetic ultrasonic anti-scaling device. Its structure is shown in Fig. 3
[19]. In fact, the device is that uses an ultra-strong alternating magnetic field and ultrasonic sound field together to process fluid. Physical morphology and chemical properties of fouling substances in oil fluids will change under the action of the magnetic field and ultrasonic field, so that they are dispersed, crushed, loose, and not easy to adhere to the pipe wall to form scale. The test results show that the advantages of the device are good scale removal effect, good antifouling effect, long life length, no pollution, convenient maintenance, and non-environmental pollution [56].

In order to solve the problems that the existing anti-wax viscosity reduction device cannot be applied to the wax content of more than 25% of the wells, Ping Zhou et al. [57] invented a double sonic eddy current anti-wax viscosity reduction device (as shown in Fig. 4 ). The innovation point of the invention is that the two-stage ultrasonic oscillator is adopted in the device to improve the acoustic frequency and destroy the crystal of the paraffin wax completely so that the effect of paraffin inhibition and viscosity reduction can be greatly increased. In addition, the eddy current technology is adopted in the device to improve the crude oil fluidity. The device can reduce the viscosity of crude oil, prevent wax deposition, increase its flow capacity and increase the production of oil wells. It is suitable for production wells of various vertical, inclined and horizontal wells [57].

In 2014, anew-type complete setofequipment for oil production was developed by Shushan Zhao in China [58,59]. The working principle of this new invention is that ultrasonic generator in the downhole system starts to work after the underground system reaches oil reservoir, and its resonant frequency drifts with the change of the liquid temperature and pressure of the underground load. The relevant information is collected through the data acquisition system to determine the load resonant frequency so that the frequency of the ultrasonic generator can be adjusted to achieve a solid-liquid coupling resonance effect. The resonance excitation can produce cracking-thermal effects, and cavitation effects in the oil reservoir, thus changing the molecular structure of oil reservoir molecules, improving the fluidity of the loaded liquid and increasing the reservoir permeability. The test results show that the invention has no environmental pollution, and has the advantages of simple operation, low cost, remarkable economic benefits, and remarkable social benefits. In addition, they have developed an ultrasonic generator. Its output power is between 0 and 100 kW, and the output frequency is between 10 kHz and 35 kHz.

To improve the transmission efficiency of the whole oil production system, three kinds of special cables, as shown in Fig. 5 , were designed by Zou Yelong et al. [60]. The cable core of the three special cables are all made of copper, the insulation of the signal line is made of cross-linked polyethylene, the signal line is twisted by seven copper wires. The protective layers are all made of cross-linked polyethylene. In the long run, a signal line made of copper wire will be gradually replaced by optical fiber due to that optical fiber has many incomparable advantages of less attenuation, small occupied space and is not sensitive to temperature.

In order to recycle oil recovery wastewater, Peng Xu et al. [61] invented an ultrasonic radiation device (its structure is shown in Fig. 6 ). It is composed of an ultrasonic generator, ultrasonic transducer, and filter core. It can quickly remove the plug of oil recovery tank filter, reduce the pollutant content of the oil recovery wastewater and break the bond of polymers and reduce their viscosity. The experimental results show that the ultrasonic radiation device not only has the characteristics of on-line processing, convenient use, lasting effect, and low energy consumption but also has a direct impact on the polymer, no secondary pollution, and low operating cost.

In 2015, Lixin Bai invented a coupling cavitation processing device for the fields of energy, environmental protection, chemical industry and medicine [62]. The apparatus comprises a fluid cavitation generator, an ultrasonic transducer, and a reaction chamber. The water outlet of the fluid cavitation generator communicates with the reaction chamber. The reaction chamber comprises a parallel disposed reflector and a radiating surface. The reflector and the radiating surface form a flat space. The reflector radiates the ultrasonic waves generated by the ultrasonic transducer. When the ultrasonic wave is reflected and superimposed repeatedly by the reflecting plate and the radiation surface, the sound field in the reaction chamber can be strengthened so as to form a large area of ultrasonic cavitation cloud spreading horizontally along the reaction chamber. The invention can realize the efficient coupling of the ultrasonic cavitation and the hydrodynamic cavitation processing technology and has much potential application values in the fields of energy, environmental protection, chemical industry, and medicine. They have developed a new type of high-power ultrasonic equipment in cooperation with Harbin land ultrasonic oil production company, and achieved good oil production results in daqing oilfield [62].

In 2013, Qiang Hu and Qiang Li invented a high-power ultrasonic oil recovery device [63]. The equipmen comprises an oil reservoir positioning device and an ultrasonic processing system. The oil reservoir positioning device comprises an oil layer positioning emitter and an oil layer positioning recognizer which are matched with each other; ultrasonic transducer positioning, positioning transmitter oil reservoir recognition device, ultrasonic and ultrasonic operation by oil cable bridle and underground are installed in the casing. The equipment cannot generate pollution and damage to the oil layer, meets the requirements of environmental protection and the sustainable development of oil fields, and has the advantages of low cost, quick effect, and high benefit [63].

In 2017, Xu Zhou invented a plug removal device for oil and gas wells [64]. The equipment consists of a short joint and nozzles, and an ultrasonic generator. The ultrasonic generator consists of a rectangular nozzle, a reed whistle, and a red clip. The ends of the rectangular nozzles are connected to the short sections and reed clamps respectively. The rectangular nozzle has an open circular tube at one end and a rectangular nozzle with a rectangular opening at the other end. The blade at one end of the reed whistle is facing the rectangular nozzle. A convex shaft is fixed at the corresponding positions on both sides of the reed whistle. The reed clamp is a strip-shaped metal member with a through a hole and is mounted on the reed clip by a convex shaft. The device utilizes ultrasonic effects such as cavitation, temperature rise, sonic flow, and microfluidic forces to remove clogging dirt from the inner wall of the wellbore, borehole or near the wellbore. In addition, sonic waves or ultrasonic waves of different frequencies can be obtained by adjusting the jet velocity of the liquid stream and the distance between the reed whistle and the rectangular nozzle.

In 2015, A device for the extraction of heavy oil was invented by Zhenlin Chen [65]. Specifically, the invention relates to a method and a device for exploiting heavy oil viscosity and breaking the molecular chain by exploiting the combined effects of sound, light, and electricity. The device is divided into underground and downhole parts. The underground includes power and controller. The downhole part includes a hook for fixing devices, an ultrasonic instrument, a light source with a wavelength of 1–500 nm, an electric heater and an oil quality monitor. The downhole part is connected to the underground part by means of cables and detecting information feedback lines. The core of the invention lies in that the ultrasonic waves with optimum frequency bands can be selected for the exploitation of different heavy oil, thus, it can make the ultrasonic waves exert its maximum effect and avoid unnecessary energy consumption, so as to reduce viscosity and break chain rapidly. Research indicated that the recovery rate can be improved by about 1–5%, using this invention, in addition, the invention has the following advantages: small footprint, suitable for all types of the reservoir exploitation in various types and stages, and simple process, low energy consumption, no pollution, having the ability to clear the plug, good orientation, convenient operation, and easy management [65].

An important branch of ultrasonic oil recovery technology is to study the changes of rock mechanics parameters and its properties under the action of ultrasonic treatment and the action of sonochemistry, which belongs to the field of ultrasound and rock mechanics. At present, the application of ultrasonic in rock mechanics is mainly focused on the ultrasonic nondestructive testing of a rock mass. The main research is the propagation low of an ultrasonic wave in the rock mass, the dynamic elastic mechanical state and structural characteristics of rock mass can be understood by this research. However, there are few studies on the changes of rock mechanics parameters and properties (constitutive relations, tensile strength, compressive strength, shear strength, elastic modulus, Poisson's ratio, etc.) under the action of ultrasonic and chemical combination treatment, which, to a certain extent, hinders the promotion and application of ultrasonic oil recovery technology [65].

In order to solve the above problem, in 2012, the apparatus and method for measuring downhole rock mechanical parameters under ultrasonic and chemical action were developed by Chunsheng Pu and Hongxin Xu [66]. The invented device comprises a sealed box body for placing the tested core, a rock mechanics testing machine for symmetrically loading the tested cores from the left and right sides through the left loading shaft and the right loading shaft, a ultrasonic transducer, an ultrasonic generator, a hydraulic booster, an exhaust valve arranged in the upper part of the closed box, a pressure detection unit, an ultrasonic power detector, an ultrasonic amplitude measuring instrument and a display unit. The experimental procedures are as follows: (1) experimental preparation; (2) adding chemical reagents; (3) Starting ultrasonic treatment equipment; (4) measuring the mechanical parameters of rock. The research found that the device has the advantages of reasonable design, convenient installation, perfect function, and convenient operation, in addition, the equipment can quickly complete the measurement of rock mechanics parameters under the combined action of ultrasonic and chemical reagents [66].

In 2015, the research teams who are committed to improving ultrasonic oil production technique in Beihang University have developed a new high-power ultrasonic transducer [61]. The piezoelectric vibrator of which is made of a new piezoelectric material-lithium niobate crystal (36°Y-Cut) due to its strong piezoelectric effect [55–59]. Fig. 7 shows that the piezoelectric vibrator is the core part of the transducer. Round electrode and ring electrode are made of copper. Insulated wire of 1.8 mm wide can be used to insulate these two electrodes. The round electrode in the middle is connected to the signal line, the area of which is wafer’s effective ultrasound radiating area. The three-dimensional model is shown in Fig. 7. This structure is designed on the way of back linings with air. One sensor has a number of lithium niobate piezoelectric vibrators, due to same emission frequency and vibration phase, ultrasonic acoustic fields exited by those lithium niobate piezoelectric vibrators are coherent wave field.
The latest typical ultrasonic oil recovery equipment developed in China is summarized in Table 1 .

ce:section.ce:section-title: Development trend of ultrasonic oil recovery technology
ce:section.ce:para: There are many kinds of composite piezoelectric ultrasonic transducers that have been developed and used, most of them are divided into a longitudinal composite, longitudinal-torsional composite, and longitudinal bending composite vibration mode according to the vibration mode.

The high-power ultrasonic transducer for oil production is a radial composite piezoelectric ultrasonic transducer. This kind of ultrasonic radiators radiates ultrasonic waves outward by generating vibration along the radius. The vibrational transducer has the advantages of simple structure, stable performance, large power capacity, large acoustic radiation area, high radiation efficiency, uniform directivity and high sensitivity along the radius. In addition to oil production, it can also be used as underwater acoustic receiver or transmitter, mostly used in underwater acoustic technology, ultrasonic technology, geology, petroleum exploration, and other fields. The shape of the radial composite piezoelectric ultrasonic transducer is mainly related to the shape of a vibrator. The common types of piezoelectric oscillators for radial vibration are a disk, tubular and annular.

Different shape transducers are used in different fields: thin-walled toroidal transducers are often used as standard hydrophones, supercharged transducers are often used to emit and receive acoustic signals, and planar radial vibration piezoelectric wafers can be used to measure the properties of materials. Tank-type ultrasonic cleaning equipment was replaced by the previous popular cleaning technology of circular tube ultrasonic transducer. In summary, radial ultrasonic transducer has a broad application prospect.

Although great progress has been made in the field of equipment development, mechanism research and industrial application, there is still considerable room for the development of ultrasonic oil recovery technology in China in recent years. The next research direction is as follows:
(1)
Develop oil layer positioning and ultrasonic integrated downhole transducers. At present, the ultrasonic operation, oil layer positioning and ultrasonic treatment need to be separately positioned next to the instrument and the ultrasonic transducer. The operation of one well requires two cables to be taken, which wastes time and increases positioning error. Therefore, there is an urgent need to develop a reservoir positioning and ultrasonic integrated downhole transducer to improve efficiency and operational success rate.
(2)
Continue to study the mechanism of ultrasonic oil recovery. Due to the complexity of the underground oil layer structure and the multidisciplinary nature of ultrasonic oil recovery technology (involving oilfield geology, oil recovery technology, logging engineering, acoustics, seepage mechanics, rock mechanics, mechanical and electrical engineering, etc.).

As far as ultrasonic oil recovery mechanism is concerned, no convincing and generally accepted conclusions have been obtained. It is impossible to reveal the mechanism of ultrasonic oil extraction in depth, lack of scientific basis and quantitative analysis, and it is difficult to determine the technical indicators of ultrasonic oil production equipment, optimize the construction operation parameters, and especially the screening of the technical operation wells, which greatly hinders the popularization and application of ultrasonic oil recovery technology. Therefore, it is not only necessary but also urgent to continue to study its mechanism in depth.

To sum up, the development of new materials can improve the efficiency of ultrasonic oil recovery processing equipment, which is the development trend of ultrasonic oil recovery technology in the future. For example, signal line made of copper wire will be gradually replaced by optical fiber due to that optical fiber has many incomparable advantages of less attenuation, small occupied space and is not sensitive to temperature; ultrasonic transducer for oil production can be designed using some new piezoelectric materials with excellent performance rather than the commonly used PZT, such as lithium niobate due to that it has good mechanical performance and chemical stability, its mechanical quality factor is obviously higher than that of ordinary piezoelectric ceramics.

Ultrasonic oil recovery technology has obvious advantages compared with traditional thermal and chemical mining methods. The application of this technology greatly improves the recovery rate while greatly reducing the mining cost, and the construction is simple and does not pollute the oil layer, so it has a broad prospect. In this paper, the concept and mechanism of ultrasonic oil recovery technology are elaborated. In addition, state-of-the-art on the development of ultrasonic oil production devices for enhanced oil recovery in China is introduced. However, due to the limited range of ultrasonic wave propagation in the reservoir, ultrasonic wave cannot play a role far away from the oil well. In order to apply this technology widely, the following aspects should be strengthened:
(1)
At present, all kinds of Optimization Research on the mechanism of ultrasonic oil production have only made great achievements in the laboratory, and have not been applied to the actual field production on a large scale. Because the laboratory experiments can not completely reflect the real formation environment, it is necessary to strengthen the practice in the future.
(2)
Currently, ultrasonic oil recovery technology only plays a large role in low-permeability and heavy oil reservoirs. In future, the oil recovery technology can be considered to be applied to the exploitation of shale gas and coalbed methane. This is a development direction with great prospects in the future;
(3)
Ultrasonic oil recovery technology, as a kind of tertiary oil recovery, has a great effect on increasing production, but it will be limited by the radius of action. In future research, ultrasonic oil recovery technology can be considered in combination with other chemical mining and thermal mining technologies to maximize the benefits of production.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research was supported by the Shanghai Natural Science Foundation under the grant number of 18ZR1426700.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ultsonch.2019.104791.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
